text: ↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡️🇮🇷🇺🇸🇮🇱 Iran’s Foreign Ministry spokesman Esmail Baqaei said the country’s armed forces are ready to deal with any movement or adventurism by the United States and Israel. While a diplomatic process continues, security forces are more vigilant than during…" ⚡️ 🇮🇷 🇺🇸 Iran’s Foreign Ministry spokesman Esmail Baqaei said that threats to assassinate a country’s negotiators should wake up the entire world. “Those who raise this issue should be prosecuted for incitement to violence and state terrorism,” he stated. “In the twenty‑first century, by a country that claims democracy and the rule of law, diplomats and negotiators are threatened with murder. This violates all principles of international law and all human and ethical standards of civilisation,” he added. @wfwitness
🇺🇸 🇮🇷 🇨🇳 Alright, this blockade lasted for a long time 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡️🇮🇷🇺🇸🇻🇦 Iran’s Foreign Ministry spokesman Esmail Baqaei reacted to President Trump’s insult to the Pope, stating that the "cowardly assassination" of Iran’s Supreme Leader – a religious authority – is a stain on America’s record that will never be forgotten.…" ⚡️ 🇮🇷 🇺🇸 🇮🇱 Iran’s Foreign Ministry spokesman Esmail Baqaei said the country’s armed forces are ready to deal with any movement or adventurism by the United States and Israel. While a diplomatic process continues, security forces are more vigilant than during wartime and are monitoring developments. “Readiness and vigilance against aggression and breach of promise must be 24/7. We must learn from past experiences and maintain our preparedness in all aspects with all our being." @wfwitness

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡️🇮🇷🇪🇺🇺🇸🇮🇱 Iran’s Foreign Ministry spokesman said Tehran is pleased that many European countries have avoided the trap set by the United States and Israel regarding the Strait of Hormuz. He added that Iran has been the guardian of security in the waterway…" ⚡️ 🇮🇷 🇺🇸 🇻🇦 Iran’s Foreign Ministry spokesman Esmail Baqaei reacted to President Trump’s insult to the Pope, stating that the "cowardly assassination" of Iran’s Supreme Leader – a religious authority – is a stain on America’s record that will never be forgotten. He warned that indifference and inaction against wrong acts will eventually affect everyone. The Pope had only called for peace and condemned the unjust war against Iran; the fact that Americans cannot tolerate even a religious leader’s request for peace shows that if the international community does not respond to continued lawlessness, consequences will follow. @wfwitness

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡️🇮🇷🇺🇸 Iran’s Foreign Ministry spokesman, Esmail Baqaei, said the other side ignored part of the understanding regarding the ceasefire from the start and broke its promises, but that does not mean Iran will retreat. The issue was pursued seriously in Islamabad…" ⚡️ 🇮🇷 🇪🇺 🇺🇸 🇮🇱 Iran’s Foreign Ministry spokesman said Tehran is pleased that many European countries have avoided the trap set by the United States and Israel regarding the Strait of Hormuz. He added that Iran has been the guardian of security in the waterway for decades, and that any outside intervention only complicates matters. “Iran, with the help of regional coastal countries, can fully handle the security and safety of this waterway, provided that US aggression and the imposed war in the region come to an end,” he said. @wfwitness

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡️🇮🇷🇺🇸 Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baghaei says the Islamic Republic, with the help of regional countries, can “well manage the security of the Strait of Hormuz.” He states this is possible provided that “interventions and the imposed war…" ⚡️ 🇮🇷 🇺🇸 Iran’s Foreign Ministry spokesman, Esmail Baqaei, said the other side ignored part of the understanding regarding the ceasefire from the start and broke its promises, but that does not mean Iran will retreat. The issue was pursued seriously in Islamabad and continues to be followed up. If part of an agreement is violated, the other side is entitled to proportionally reduce its commitments. Claims that Iran has been indifferent to other fronts despite its ceasefire commitments are absolutely untrue, he added. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇨🇳 🇮🇷 US President Donald Trump says China is “very happy” with efforts to permanently open the Strait of Hormuz, adding that “this situation will never happen again.” He claims China has “agreed not to send weapons to Iran” and says President Xi Jinping will give him “a big, fat hug” during an upcoming visit. Trump adds the US is “working together smartly, and very well,” while warning that the US remains “very good at fighting” if necessary. @wfwitness

Trump: China is very happy that I am permanently opening the Strait of Hormuz. I am doing it for them, also – and the World. They have agreed not to send weapons to Iran. President Xi will give me a big, fat hug when I get there. We are working together smartly and very well. Doesn’t that beat fighting? But remember, we are very good at fighting if we have to, far better than anyone else.

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡️🇮🇷🇺🇸 Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baghaei says Iran has “not yet reached the decision-making stage” in ongoing discussions, including on the nuclear issue. He states that “multiple options” remain under consideration, noting that previous…" ⚡️ 🇮🇷 🇺🇸 Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baghaei says the Islamic Republic, with the help of regional countries, can “well manage the security of the Strait of Hormuz.” He states this is possible provided that “interventions and the imposed war by the United States in the region come to an end.” The remarks come amid ongoing tensions over maritime security and control of the strategic waterway. @wfwitness

🇺🇸 ⚡️ 🇮🇷 ABC News Chief Washington Correspondent Jonathan Karl: "President Trump told me today he isn’t thinking about extending the ceasefire. He doesn’t think it will be necessary." Trump to Karl: "I think you’re going to be watching an amazing two days ahead."
⚡ 🇮🇱 Red alert sirens are sounding in the Upper Galilee region, Northern Israel. @wfwitness
Hungary's Péter Magyar spots Viktor Orbán chilling on a balcony at President Tamás Sulyok’s residence: “Absolute cinema.”

⚡️ 🇮🇷 🇺🇸 Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baghaei says Iran has “not yet reached the decision-making stage” in ongoing discussions, including on the nuclear issue. He states that “multiple options” remain under consideration, noting that previous approaches such as the JCPOA framework have been tested but not finalised. Baghaei adds that “many previous understandings have been set aside” due to US breaches of commitments and recent military developments. @wfwitness

🇮🇷 ⚡️ 🇺🇸 Another US-sanctioned Crude Oil Tanker, Curacao-flagged VLCC tanker ALICIA, with a history of Iranian oil shipments, entered the Persian Gulf today via Iran's inner route in the Strait of Hormuz despite blockade threats.

📸 German Chancellor Merz and Zelensky checking out Auterion-Airlogix joint venture's Anubis kamikaze drone. Anubis has a Shahed-style layout. The company was created to mass-produce AI-guided autonomous strike drones for Ukraine and NATO. Germany ordered thousands of these drones for Ukraine.

🇮🇷 Commander of Iran’s Khatam al-Anbiya Central Headquarters issued a firm warning to the United States: ➡️ If the U.S. continues the unlawful maritime pressure and blockade activities in the region, and persists in endangering Iranian commercial and oil shipping, it will be seen as a violation of the current ceasefire framework. ➡️ Iran states it will not allow normal import and export shipping to proceed under conditions of insecurity in the Persian Gulf, the Sea of Oman, or the Red Sea if these actions continue. ➡️ Tehran emphasizes it will act decisively to defend its sovereignty and economic interests. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

🇱🇧 Hassan Fadlallah: Bint Jbeil stands firm; retreat is not in our dictionary. 🔹 The Hezbollah representative in the Lebanese Parliament, criticizing the Lebanese government's performance, said: Retreat has no meaning in the lexicon of the Resistance, and Bint Jbeil continues to stand with the unparalleled courage of the Resistance fighters

🇷🇺 🇪🇺 🇺🇳 Russian UN Ambassador Vassily Nebenzia accused the EU of preparing a major war against Russia during a UN Security Council meeting on April 13, 2026. The meeting was the annual session on UN-EU cooperation . Nebenzia used it to deliver sharp criticism of the bloc and also took the time to address the EU Chief Foreign Affairs Embarrassment Kaka Kallas directly. His central claims were: ➡️ Accusation of War Preparations : He stated that increased European defense spending proves Europe is " seriously preparing for a major war against Russia" . ➡️ Providing Evidence: He cited that EU defense spending reached €381 billion last year, a nearly 60% increase, ranking second only to the US. ➡️ Threat of Response: Nebenzia warned that Russia would be forced to respond to these " anti-Russian attacks, " adding that no conqueror who came to Russia "with the sword" has ever left unscathed . ➡️ He also accused the EU of sabotaging peace efforts in Ukraine and escalating the conflict by supplying weapons. ➡️ Apart from calling out EU's preparations for a mayor war, he called Brussels a New Reich, and mocked Kaka Kallas’s history knowledge — all in one speech. Vassily Nebenzia: “These large-scale military preparations leave no doubt that Europe is seriously engaged in preparations for a major war against Russia. This is nothing more than playing with fire since we will be forced to react to anti-Russian attacks in a mirror manner. And none of the conquerors who came to us with a sword, and there are countless of them, left Russia in good health. Such an overtly militaristic course is far less surprising if one looks closely at those responsible for formulating it. Some comments are so absurd and ridiculous that it would be quite appropriate to raise the question of the total professional unsuitability of their authors. In particular, the head of European diplomacy, speaking at a conference of the EU Institute for Security Studies last year, commenting on Vladimir Putin’s speech at the celebrations in China, said the following: ‘Russia turned to China and said, Russia and China fought in World War II. We won World War II. We defeated Nazism. And I thought, This is something new.’ If you know the story, it raises a lot of questions. And this is said by the EU High Representative for Foreign Affairs, who obviously knows nothing about the fact that it was the peoples of the Soviet Union and the Celestial Empire who took the brunt of the Second World War, suffering heavy human losses. Still, we would very much like to meet the history teacher of Ms. Kallas. To sum up, I would like to say that apparently the EU is moving from the beautiful post-war idea of a united Europe in transit through the blooming garden to the construction of another Reich. We hope that both in the European countries themselves and in the international community as a whole, there are still sober heads who understand the risks and are ready to prevent such a development of events. Thank you for your attention.” 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡️🇺🇸🇮🇷 U.S. President Trump said the conflict with Iran is “really about no nuclear” and that Iran cannot have a nuclear weapon. “If they’re going to have a nuclear weapon, we’ll be living with them for a little while, but I don’t know how much longer they…" ⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 U.S. President Trump said the reaction to the U.S. blockade of the Strait of Hormuz has been “pretty amazing”, adding that Iran seems more reactive now than before. He claimed Iran has no navy, no air force, no anti‑aircraft equipment and no radar, and that its leaders are gone. “The leaders they have now… we find them pretty reasonable, to be honest with you,” he said. Trump reiterated that the core issue is preventing Iran from obtaining a nuclear weapon. He said the U.S. could destroy all of Iran’s bridges and power plants in one hour, but rebuilding would take years. He reported no pushback from China or Saudi Arabia over the blockade. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 Commander of Iran’s Khatam al-Anbiya Central Headquarters Aliabadi warned that Iran would threaten trade in the Red Sea if the U.S. blockade of the Strait of Hormuz continues. “If the aggressive and terrorist America wants to continue its illegal action of maritime blockade in the region and create insecurity for Iranian commercial ships and oil tankers, this American action will be a prelude to the violation of the ceasefire. If the maritime blockade of the region by the United States continues, the powerful Iranian armed forces will not allow any exports or imports to continue in the Persian Gulf, the Sea of Oman, and the Red Sea.” @wfwitness

BREAKING: Commander of Iran’s Khatam al-Anbiya Headquarters: We will not allow any export or import activity in the Gulf and the Sea of Oman if the American blockade continues. Our armed forces will not allow trade to flow through the Red Sea if the naval blockade continues. If America continues its naval blockade, this will be considered a prelude to violating the ceasefire.
BIG: Mediators say the U.S. and Iran have agreed “in principle” to extend the current ceasefire beyond April 22 to allow more negotiations. Source: AP

⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 U.S. President Trump said the conflict with Iran is “really about no nuclear” and that Iran cannot have a nuclear weapon. “If they’re going to have a nuclear weapon, we’ll be living with them for a little while, but I don’t know how much longer they can survive,” he said, adding that an end to the conflict could come very soon. @wfwitness

🇮🇷 ⚡️ 🇺🇸 OFAC-Sanctioned Iranian Vessel Golbon Continues Transit Near Strait of Hormuz Despite Blockade U.S. Central Command has repeatedly stated that “no ships made it past the US blockade”. However, independent maritime tracking data tells a more nuanced story. Several Iran-linked or previously sanctioned vessels, including the Golbon, still “slipped” shortly after the blockade started.
